examples.text: وحدات من الجيش السوري دخلت الى مدينة طفس 10كم شمال غرب درعا وسط ترحيب من الأهالي بعد انضمام البلدة الى اتفاق المصالحة ،و الذي ينص على تسليم السلاح الثقيل والمتوسط ،نتيجة لضغط العملية العسكرية التي يقوم بها الجيش السوري في ريف درعا الغربي.
examples.english: Units of the Syrian army enter in to the city Ṭafas 10 km north of Darʿā amidst the welcoming population after the accession to the reconciliation agreement, which stipulates surrender of heavy and medium firearms, as a result of pressure from military activity which the Syrian army has under taken in the western surrounding area.

body.text: of a thing: this is the primary signification. (Az, TA.) It is said in a trad., (S, M,) of 'Alee, (S,)
, (K, * TA,) but the former is that which is commonly known; (TA;) i. e.
When women attain the period of mature intellect,
and know the real natures of things,
then the male relations on the father's side have a better right to dispose of her
when they attain to that age at which they are qualified to contend for their rights;
[accord. to the former reading;] for this is what is termed
when they attain to that period at which they become objects of contention for right; when every one of the guardians asserts himself to have the best right:
in the trad. is a metaphorical term, from the same word as applied to camels [when entering upon the fourth year]; (K, * TA;) and
, also, in this case, accord. to some, properly signifies the same, being a pl. of
when they attain to the extreme term of childhood.
(M, K.)       -A2-  In the conventional language of men of science, it signifies (tropical:)
in the sense of the measure
of the Kur-án, and of the traditions, is
a sentence, indicating a particular meaning, not admitting any other than it:
(TA:) so in the conventional language of the lawyers and the scholastic theologians: (MF, on the
an ordinance, indicated by the manifest
of the Kur-án, and of the Sunneh: from
as denoting “ elevation ” and “ appearance: ” or, as some say, from
as signifying “ he elicited, and made apparent, his opinion. ” (TA.) Hence, also, as used by the practical lawyers, it signifies (tropical:)
of the Sunneh, used as an authority in an argument, for proof of an assertion.
] The pl. [in all these senses] is
of an author, book, writing, or passage: frequently used in this sense.]       -A3-
of an affair, or a state, or case. (M, TA.)       -A4-
A vehement pace, in which a beast is made to exert itself to the full,
in which the utmost possible celerity is elicited;
[this meaning seems to be indicated, though not expressed, in the S;] syn.
: (K:) or, the former, as Az says, in one place,
or, as he says in another place, the
utmost pace which a beast of carriage is able to attain:
